# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { ReadableStream } from 'stream/web';

export const config = {
  runtime: 'edge',
};

export default async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  if (req.method !== 'POST') {
    return new Response('Method Not Allowed', { status: 405 });
  }

  try {
    const { content, headers: responseHeaders } = await req.json();

    if (!content) {
      return new Response('Content is required', { status: 400 });
    }

    const stream = new ReadableStream({
      async start(controller) {
        try {
          if (typeof content === 'string') {
            controller.enqueue(new TextEncoder().encode(content));
          } else if (Array.isArray(content)) {
            for (const chunk of content) {
              controller.enqueue(new TextEncoder().encode(chunk));
            }
          } else {
            controller.enqueue(new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(content)));
          }
          controller.close();
        } catch (error) {
          controller.error(error);
        }
      },
    });

    const headers = new Headers();
    
    if (responseHeaders && typeof responseHeaders === 'object') {
      Object.entries(responseHeaders).forEach(([key, value]) => {
        if (typeof value === 'string') {
          headers.append(key, value);
        }
      });
    }

    headers.set('Content-Type', 'text/plain; charset=utf-8');
    headers.set('Transfer-Encoding', 'chunked');

    return new Response(stream, {
      headers,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response('Invalid JSON', { status: 400 });
  }
}
